A REST API (Representational State Transfer Application Programming Interface) is a set of rules for how software applications interact over the web. It allows different services to communicate with each other using standard HTTP methods like GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE. Each resource in a REST API has a unique URL, and data is typically returned in formats like JSON or XML.
